name: Pork Stew with Cornmeal Dumplings
recipeInstructions:
-----------------------------CORNMEAL DUMPLINGS-----------------------------
1/2 cup Flour
1/2 cup Cheddar cheese
1/3 cup Cornmeal (yellow)
1 tsp Baking powder
Dash Pepper
1 Egg (beaten)
2 tbsp Milk
2 tbsp Oil
2 tbsp Cheddar cheese (shredded)
Cut the pork into 1-inch cubes. In a large skillet, brown the pork and garlic in hot oil. Drain well.
Meanwhile, in the crockpot combine carrots, potatoes, water, tapioca, sugar, Worcestershire sauce, bay leaves, thyme, salt, nutmeg, and pepper. Stir in the browned meat and undrained tomatoes. Cover; cook on low-heat setting for 9-11 hours or on high-heat setting for 4-5 hours.
If stew was cooked on low-heat setting, turn the crockpot to high-heat setting. Prepare cornmeal dumplings. Remove bay leaves. Stir the stew and drop dumplings by tablespoonfuls onto the stew. Cover; cook for an additional 50 minutes (do not lift the cover). Sprinkle dumplings with cheese.
DUMPLINGS: In a medium mixing bowl, stir together flour, cheddar cheese, cornmeal, baking powder, and pepper. Combine beaten egg, milk, and oil. Add to flour mixture; stir with a fork just until combined. Use in Pork stew with cornmeal dumplings above.
